entries.requirement: Clark begins as a Mac-first application with a permanent desktop process and security topology.

evidence.claim: Defines Studio, main, renderer, Harness, media workers, Keychain, Share capture, and direct Mac distribution topology.
Accepts the Mac-first Electron topology and privileged process boundaries.
Accepts Developer ID, notarization, Hardened Runtime, and the Share-extension boundary.

entries.acceptanceRule: Ground requires an accepted permanent Mac topology. Signed runtime, entitlements, notarization, native QA, and release evidence belong to Stratum 1.
limitations: No signed Mac runtime exists yet.
entries.nextAction: Build the signed Stratum 1 vertical slice through the accepted process topology without creating a browser-only production path.

entries.requirement: Clark has a provider-neutral durable Harness with explicit loops, checkpoints, budgets, recovery, and bounded agent work.

evidence.claim: Defines durable run, step, recovery, agent, and provider-neutral execution behavior.
Accepts a durable Harness above replaceable providers.
Versions loop graphs, gates, permissions, budgets, and policies.
Compiles the Full-Week loop into an eleven-step plan with approval, egress, budget, idempotency, and reconciliation data.
Validates both loops, every compiled step, DAG integrity, permissions, budget, gates, and publishing safety.

entries.acceptanceRule: Ground requires one coherent and executable contract model; production restart, chaos, cost, and provider conformance proof belongs to later strata.
limitations: No production run engine or forced-recovery test exists.
entries.nextAction: Generate runtime types and implement the event-backed run state machine in Stratum 2.

entries.requirement: The Focus and Canvas interaction model is proven strong enough before implementation fans out.

evidence.claim: Implements Focus, the 50-object Canvas, lineage, impact, comparison, memory, Connections, keyboard control, and reversible staged state.
Records controlled-browser interaction, runtime, and 320/390 responsive checks.
Records five expert workflow rehearsals while explicitly excluding them from participant evidence.

entries.acceptanceRule: Five representative creators must pass the precommitted Canvas rubric; expert review and automated checks cannot substitute for observed behavior.
limitations: Zero representative creator sessions have been recorded.
entries.nextAction: Run five observed walkthroughs, synthesize the matrix, redesign every failed critical gate, and retest before Ground signoff.

entries.requirement: Clark consumes MCP servers through a capability contract that remains safe across transports and providers.

evidence.claim: Defines Clark as an MCP host/client with negotiation, trust, lifecycle, and capability adaptation.
Places the Clark capability contract above MCP transport semantics.
Versions capability inputs, outputs, permissions, cost, approval, async, trust, and lifecycle.
Represents a read-only research MCP capability.
Validates eight capability revisions and their use in loops and the compiled run plan.

entries.acceptanceRule: Ground requires versioned capability semantics and representative valid/invalid fixtures; hostile live-server conformance is a separate blocking delivery item.
limitations: No hostile stdio/HTTP MCP conformance runner exists.
entries.nextAction: Produce the hostile MCP plan and then implement it with exact-command, scope, revocation, timeout, and malformed-response fixtures.

entries.requirement: Clark exposes its own scoped MCP server, Clark Bridge, over the same canonical project state used by Studio.

evidence.claim: Defines Bridge clients, tools, resources, prompts, scopes, jobs, and state-equivalence invariant.
Requires Clark to consume and expose MCP through canonical domain commands and queries.
Connections explains Bridge surface, clients, scopes, and one canonical workspace.

entries.acceptanceRule: Before implementation fans out, Bridge tool/resource schemas and a clickable Studio-versus-Bridge state-equivalence scenario must exist and pass without a second state model.
limitations: No Bridge tool/resource schema fixture exists.
The prototype describes Bridge but cannot issue a Bridge command and show the identical Studio state.
entries.nextAction: Add versioned Bridge schemas, a command/receipt fixture, and an interactive state-equivalence scenario to the prototype and verifier.

entries.requirement: Clark connects creator work to broad social distribution through Postiz-first, selective direct adapters, and permanent export fallback.

evidence.claim: Defines Postiz-first breadth, direct strategic adapters, reconciliation, capability state, and export fallback.
Accepts the layered distribution strategy without claiming universal direct support.
Represents social publish and observation capabilities with permissions and approval.
Defines versioned Postiz publication behavior for the representative loop.
Proves publish approval, idempotency, reconciliation, egress, and capability references at contract level.

entries.acceptanceRule: Ground requires honest integration architecture and contract fixtures; real platform approval, account matrices, and reliability are later release evidence.
limitations: No real social account has been connected or mutated.
entries.nextAction: Build recorded/sandbox adapter fixtures and the real-account capability matrix in the distribution stratum.

entries.requirement: Installable and learned skills are governed packages with quarantine, effective permissions, tests, promotion, rollback, and a real executable sandbox boundary.

evidence.claim: Defines skill sources, package shape, proposals, tests, promotion, regression, trust, and rollback.
Accepts declarative skills by default and Wasmtime/WASI for untrusted executable components.
Connections exposes trusted, quarantined, and learned skill states.

entries.acceptanceRule: Ground requires a versioned skill package schema plus a hostile Wasmtime/WASI prototype proving deny-all capability behavior and resource termination.
limitations: No skill package schema exists.
No executable Wasmtime/WASI hostile-component evidence exists.
entries.nextAction: Define the package contract, build the Class B sandbox spike, and run traversal, network, environment, resource, and credential canaries.

entries.requirement: Memory and personalization are the governed product core, with evidence, contradiction, scope, confidence, correction, forgetting, and explicit promotion.

evidence.claim: Defines the structured creator model, retrieval, proposals, correction, forgetting, reflection, and safety evaluation.
Prohibits silent learning and accepts evidence-bound, reversible memory/skill evolution.
Shows active/proposed/disputed memory, evidence, confidence, contradiction, scope, retrieval, promotion, and rejection.
Records memory inspection and promotion interaction checks.

entries.acceptanceRule: Representative creators must understand memory evidence and authority, and later real-corpus evaluation must prove helpful retrieval without leakage or poisoning.
limitations: No creator has been observed using the memory surface.
No real creator corpus evaluation exists.
entries.nextAction: Measure memory comprehension in the five walkthroughs, then define the retrieval/leakage/correction/forget evaluation corpus.

entries.requirement: Clark is planned as one whole product rather than a throwaway MVP or disconnected feature set.

evidence.claim: Defines cumulative product strata from Ground through Team without an alternate MVP architecture.
Accepts permanent contracts and cumulative delivery across the whole product.

entries.acceptanceRule: Every later stratum must extend the same domain, security, event, capability, and evidence paths; this is re-proven at each release.
limitations: Whole-product implementation evidence does not exist before implementation.
entries.nextAction: Enforce Definition of Ready/Done and reject any prototype-only production shortcut during each stratum.

entries.requirement: The architecture grows incrementally from Ground through local, intelligence, distribution, remote, and team strata.

evidence.claim: Defines ordered strata with cumulative entry and exit evidence.
Requires each stratum to preserve and prove the permanent base.

entries.acceptanceRule: A higher stratum cannot start by bypassing an unproven lower trust, state, or product invariant.
limitations: No implementation stratum has executed yet.
entries.nextAction: Close the Ground blockers and obtain explicit signoff before committed Stratum 1 implementation begins.

entries.requirement: A new creator can run the Full-Week loop from Focus without understanding graphs.

evidence.claim: Focus begins with one bounded decision, context packet, run contract, evidence, impact, and continuation.
Angle selection and continuation interactions pass.

entries.acceptanceRule: At least four of five representative creators complete the Focus task without critical assistance under the walkthrough protocol.
limitations: No participant completion data exists.
entries.nextAction: Run task 1 in five observed sessions and record assistance, wrong turns, completion, and explanation.

entries.requirement: An expert identifies source, version, cost, approval, publication state, and outcome lineage for a post within ten seconds.

evidence.claim: Selecting a post exposes the complete provenance contract and bounded lineage.
Representative publication selection exposes every required field.

entries.acceptanceRule: At least four of five creators identify every field correctly within ten measured seconds without critical assistance.
limitations: No human timing data exists.
entries.nextAction: Time the lineage task in every walkthrough and redesign if the threshold or comprehension rule fails.

entries.requirement: Editing an angle explains downstream invalidation and cost before any paid regeneration.

evidence.claim: Impact preview marks downstream Production, Distribution, and Outcomes and quotes $14.60 before mutation.
Impact state is interactive, visible, and reversible.

entries.acceptanceRule: At least four of five creators correctly predict affected branches and understand that spend has not occurred.
limitations: No participant comprehension data exists.
entries.nextAction: Run the impact-prediction task and record whether cost, staleness, and reversibility are understood.

entries.requirement: Comparing two scripts and two reels is easier than coordinating the same decision across separate tools.

evidence.claim: Review provides one Reel playhead, evidence/cost/source/policy/impact comparison, notes, selection, and approval.
Play/Pause updates both surfaces and the comparison remains responsive.

entries.acceptanceRule: Representative creators complete the comparison correctly with fewer wrong turns and lower stated reconstruction effort than their current workflow.
limitations: Cards are representative rather than real participant media.
No comparative timing or effort evidence exists.
entries.nextAction: Use representative creator media in observed comparison tasks and record time, wrong turns, confidence, and current-tool baseline.

entries.requirement: A memory proposal exposes its evidence and can be corrected, rejected, or promoted in place.

evidence.claim: Memory items expose evidence and explicit Promote/Reject actions without silent activation.
Inspection and promotion interactions pass and proposal count changes correctly.

entries.acceptanceRule: At least four of five creators distinguish proposal from active memory and complete the intended governance action without critical assistance.
limitations: No observed creator governance behavior exists.
entries.nextAction: Run the memory task with an intentionally weak proposal and record whether participants inspect evidence before acting.

entries.requirement: A 50-object project remains legible through Focus, lanes, pan/zoom, filters, bounded lineage, collapse, and keyboard navigation.

evidence.claim: Renders the exact 50-object fixture across six semantic lanes with focus, filtering, pan/zoom/fit, collapse, and keyboard movement.
Records 50 nodes, drag-pan, zoom/undo, keyboard selection, and 320/390 page-width checks.

entries.acceptanceRule: At least four of five creators orient, find the requested object, and explain hidden-versus-visible structure without critical assistance.
limitations: No participant orientation or discoverability data exists.
VoiceOver is untested.
entries.nextAction: Run the orientation task with pointer and keyboard paths and record hidden-edge misunderstandings and All Structure discoverability.

entries.requirement: The same project can be operated through Studio and Clark Bridge without divergent state or authority.

evidence.claim: Connections describes Bridge clients, scopes, tools/resources/prompts, and one canonical workspace.
Requires Bridge and Studio to call the same domain commands and queries.

entries.acceptanceRule: A versioned Bridge command fixture must create a receipt and update the exact same visible project state; UI state must remain identical after refresh/replay.
limitations: Bridge is explanatory only in the prototype.
No Bridge command schema, receipt, or state-equivalence check exists.
entries.nextAction: Add the Bridge command/receipt contract and an interactive state-equivalence scenario before closing the Canvas gate.

entries.requirement: Before promotion, a creator can inspect evidence, contradiction, confidence, scope, and retrieval state behind a memory proposal.

evidence.claim: Memory inspection exposes all five required dimensions before promotion.
Records inspection state and evidence-preserving promotion behavior.

entries.acceptanceRule: At least four of five creators correctly explain confidence versus contradiction and proposal-only retrieval before promotion.
limitations: No observed comprehension evidence exists.
entries.nextAction: Ask participants to explain each memory field aloud before permitting Promote or Reject.

entries.requirement: Before a run, the creator understands automatic, budget-bound, always-ask, prohibited, and off-Mac data behavior.

evidence.claim: Focus states the run contract and remote-data boundary; Connections states effective permission intersection.
Records visible automatic, approval, prohibition, budget, and egress categories.

entries.acceptanceRule: At least four of five creators correctly classify each action and identify what data may leave the Mac without facilitator correction.
limitations: No observed authority or egress comprehension evidence exists.
entries.nextAction: Run the authority classification task and redesign any category that participants confuse.

entries.requirement: At least ten target creators independently identify continuity and coordination as a costly current problem.

evidence.claim: Defines the target creator, replacement target, and precommitted interview gate.

entries.acceptanceRule: Ten target creators describe the costly problem without being led; feature enthusiasm does not count.
limitations: No target-creator interview records exist.
entries.nextAction: Recruit and conduct ten problem/replacement interviews with behavioral examples and current-tool costs.

entries.requirement: Five target creators complete the Canvas walkthrough and understand lineage, authority, memory evidence, and Connections.

evidence.claim: Defines recruitment, tasks, timing, assistance, thresholds, analysis, and redesign rules.

entries.acceptanceRule: Five completed session records and one analysis matrix must meet every precommitted critical threshold.
limitations: No participant record exists.
entries.nextAction: Run and record five sessions using the existing protocol and templates.

entries.requirement: At least three design partners operate a representative real multi-channel week using their content and current tools.

evidence.claim: Defines participant criteria, baseline, week operation, metrics, failures, memory burden, and weekly evidence.

entries.acceptanceRule: Three qualified partners complete representative weeks; demo content or free-form feedback does not count.
limitations: No design partner has been recruited or recorded.
entries.nextAction: Recruit three qualified partners and schedule baseline plus four weekly evidence reviews.

entries.requirement: Creators choose Clark over their prior coordination layer for four consecutive weeks because reconstruction work is materially lower.

evidence.claim: Defines recurring replacement-value and coordination-reconstruction measurement.

entries.acceptanceRule: Observed return behavior and baseline deltas persist for four weeks; stated intent alone does not count.
limitations: No longitudinal usage evidence exists.
entries.nextAction: Track return, prior-tool fallback, coordination time, reconstruction time, and reasons for four consecutive weeks.

entries.requirement: At least three target creators make a binding purchase action at a sustainable package and price.

evidence.claim: Defines acceptable binding signals, disclosure, recording, and precommitted pass/fail rules.

entries.acceptanceRule: Three binding actions are required and at least two occur without a custom feature promise that changes the product core.
limitations: No deposit, paid pilot, order, or equivalent binding action exists.
entries.nextAction: Run the purchase protocol only after representative use and record offer, price, action, conditions, and later cancellation/refund.

entries.requirement: Target creators explain Clark's value without relying on the words canvas, MCP, or a model vendor.

evidence.claim: Defines creator continuity and the decision graph as the value thesis above commodity features.

entries.acceptanceRule: Participants independently describe continuity, retained judgment, coordination, provenance, or compounding capability rather than a technical feature label.
limitations: No participant language evidence exists.
entries.nextAction: Ask every walkthrough and design partner to explain Clark to a peer without prompting and code the response.

entries.requirement: Provider spend, support, distribution, and Relay costs support a sustainable package without hidden fees or data lock-in.

evidence.claim: Defines packaging, pass-through spend, margin hypotheses, and sustainability gate.

entries.acceptanceRule: Measured usage and support cost at accepted prices must leave a sustainable package while preserving export and clear provider-spend separation.
limitations: No measured usage, support, Relay, or accepted-price cohort exists.
entries.nextAction: Capture cost and support data during design-partner weeks and test it against the exact binding offer.

entries.requirement: Every owning workstream reviews and signs off the v1 event, loop, capability, run-plan, failure, and fixture contracts.

evidence.claim: Documents the current contract set and reproducible verifier but contains no cross-team approval record.

entries.acceptanceRule: Each producer and consumer owner signs the same v1 semantics; no private dialect or unresolved compatibility objection remains.
limitations: No named workstream signoff exists.
entries.nextAction: Run a contract review with W2-W10, record decisions and required changes, rerun fixtures, and capture named approvals.

entries.requirement: Generated implementation types and the event upcaster/schema-evolution approach are selected without forking contract semantics.

evidence.claim: Defines versioned event envelopes but no generated types or upcaster implementation choice.

entries.acceptanceRule: A repeatable generator produces the chosen language types, schema drift fails CI, and an upcaster fixture proves one historical migration.
limitations: No generated types, drift check, or upcaster fixture exists.
entries.nextAction: Choose the TypeScript generation/upcaster toolchain, commit generated artifacts and one migration fixture, and obtain Domain/Architecture signoff.

entries.requirement: A concrete hostile MCP and capability conformance plan names cases, tools, owners, severity, and blocking policy.

evidence.claim: Defines the boundary and risks but not an owned executable conformance test plan.

entries.acceptanceRule: The plan covers hostile stdio/HTTP servers, malformed schemas/results, auth/scope, timeout, cancellation, replay, secret/egress canaries, UI state, and quarantine.
limitations: No test-case inventory, harness choice, named owner, or severity policy exists.
entries.nextAction: Write the conformance specification and assign Connect, Security, and Quality owners before MCP implementation begins.

entries.requirement: ADR-0011 through ADR-0020 have owned executable security test specifications and named incident-response roles.

evidence.claim: Maps threats and accepted security decisions to verification gates and remaining implementation evidence.
Defines disclosure, support, incident, and responder requirements.

entries.acceptanceRule: Every ADR verification gate maps to a named test owner, environment, tool, severity/blocking rule, artifact destination, and responder; private reporting is enabled before beta.
limitations: Owners are roles rather than named people.
Executable test specifications and incident responders are not assigned.
entries.nextAction: Create the threat/ADR-to-test plan, assign primary/backup owners, and rehearse the reporting path before beta.

entries.requirement: A Mac-native interaction and accessibility prototype covers menu roles, VoiceOver, keyboard conventions, window behavior, and native trust prompts.

evidence.claim: The web prototype demonstrates keyboard semantics and responsive interaction but is not a signed native Mac interaction artifact.

entries.acceptanceRule: The native prototype passes keyboard and VoiceOver task checks and demonstrates menu, window, Share, notification, Keychain/TCC, and update trust language.
limitations: No Electron/Swift native prototype or VoiceOver evidence exists.
entries.nextAction: Build a bounded signed Mac interaction shell using the accepted topology and run keyboard/VoiceOver/native-prompt checks before full Studio implementation.

entries.requirement: Named people, capacity, dates, dependencies, and backups are assigned to all leadership roles and ten workstreams.

evidence.claim: Defines roles, workstreams, dependencies, contracts, readiness, done, cadence, and evidence—but intentionally contains no invented staffing.

entries.acceptanceRule: Every role/workstream has one named accountable person, realistic capacity/date, critical dependency commitment, and backup/escalation path.
limitations: No people, capacity, or dates are assigned.
entries.nextAction: Product leadership must assign the real team and record capacity/dependency commitments without collapsing accountabilities.

entries.requirement: The Wasmtime/WASI Class B skill sandbox is exercised against hostile components and resource-exhaustion cases.

evidence.claim: Selects the sandbox architecture and verification gates but is not executable evidence.

entries.acceptanceRule: Traversal, symlink escape, undeclared imports, network, environment, process, resource, terminal, and credential canaries fail within declared limits and produce receipts.
limitations: No Wasmtime embedding, WIT host interface, hostile component, or resource-metering result exists.
entries.nextAction: Build the narrow Class B spike and malicious fixture suite before installable executable skills enter implementation.

entries.requirement: The Ground evidence ledger is reviewed and signed by Product, Design, Architecture, Security, Quality, Release, and Research.

evidence.claim: Defines the versioned evidence-ledger contract.
Fails closed on missing proof, missing files, fabricated verification, stale gate state, and incomplete signoff.

entries.acceptanceRule: All required roles approve after blocking entries pass, or explicitly approve a bounded risk under the escalation rule; the gate cannot self-close from document creation.
limitations: The ledger is unsigned and contains open blockers.
entries.nextAction: Resolve blocking entries, rerun verification, then record named approvals without editing evidence status beyond what artifacts prove.
